title: Distinguish Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox.allow/2 vs Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow/3
prompt: Write `docs/allow_deep_dive.md` that clearly distinguishes the two `allow` functions at the API level AND the underlying mechanism level. Must explain: (1) `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow(repo, owner_pid, allowed_pid)` directly registers `allowed_pid` in the sandbox GenServer's state as allowed to use owner_pid's connection, (2) `Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox.allow(metadata, adapter)` takes an HTTP-header-encoded metadata map (containing the owner pid) and looks up the current process — it's the HTTP-endpoint variant, (3) the first is for PID-to-PID allowance (tests, child processes), (4) the second is for HTTP-driven allowance (LiveView sockets, channel joins, browser driver tests with metadata in User-Agent), (5) using one in place of the other produces confusing errors.
tier_rationale: Deep API comparison — few sources explain both mechanisms in one place.
